info.title: Mekhan API
info.description:
SOP workflow management â€” Petri-net backed, real-time collaborative editing.

Routes are organized by domain: templates (the visual workflow editor'ssaved state), instances (running workflows), processes (HPI observabilityover running instances), catalogue (artifact registry), and provenance(token-level causality across nets).

The JSON API lives under `/api/v1/*`; `/healthz` is the unauthenticatedliveness probe. The Yjs WebSocket endpoint at `/api/yjs/{template_id}`carries the collaborative editor's binary CRDT protocol and isintentionally not modeled here.

post.description:
Reset seeded demos to pristine: remove every seeded family (force) then
re-seed all demos from the configured on-disk demos directory. Overwrites
any user edits â€” true "reset to factory".

403.description: Editor of the default workspace required

post.description:
Remove every seeded demo family (cancelling running instances + purging
their engine nets). Does **not** re-seed â€” use `reseed` for that.

403.description: Editor of the default workspace required

post.summary:
Stateless shape analysis: the editor's single source of truth for guard
scope + diagnostics. Independent of `compile_to_air` succeeding (no files
needed) so feedback lands while editing, not at publish.

parameters.name: scope
parameters.description:
Scope context for downward-visibility resolution. `workspace` (the
caller's workspace) when omitted. Format: `workspace`, `project:<uuid>`,
or `template:<uuid>`.

post.summary: `POST /api/v1/asset-types` â€” define a new schema. Editor-or-above gated.

put.summary:
`PUT /api/v1/asset-types/{id}` â€” additive-only schema update (docs/20 Â§4.3).
A `fields` change bumps `version` only after passing the additive gate.

delete.summary:
`DELETE /api/v1/asset-types/{id}` â€” soft delete. Rejected when any live
asset still references the type (cascade-guard, docs/20 Â§8).

post.summary:
`POST /api/v1/assets` â€” create an empty asset of a given type. Records are
written separately via `PUT /records` or `POST /import-csv`.

get.summary: `GET /api/v1/assets/{id}` â€” metadata + a page of the current-version records.

delete.summary:
`DELETE /api/v1/assets/{id}` â€” soft delete. Records stay (CASCADE only on
hard delete) so already-pinned instances keep resolving.

post.summary:
`POST /api/v1/assets/{id}/files` â€” upload a file for a `File` field â†’ S3,
returns the storage path to embed in a record's JSONB (docs/20 Â§4.1). The
dual-source File model also accepts a catalogue-entry `storage_path` as a
bare string; that path is reused verbatim (no copy) so this endpoint is only
for fresh uploads.

parameters.name: field
parameters.description: The File field name this upload is for

post.summary:
`POST /api/v1/assets/{id}/import-csv` â€” parse a CSV multipart body, map
columns to the type's fields, coerce per `FieldKind`, validate, and write a
new version (replace or append per `?append=`).

parameters.name: has_header
parameters.description:
When `true`, the first CSV row is treated as a header row whose cells are
field names. When `false`, columns map positionally to the type's field
order. Default `true`.

parameters.name: append
parameters.description:
When `true`, append the imported rows to the current version; default
`false` = replace.

put.summary:
`PUT /api/v1/assets/{id}/records` â€” replace (or append) records. Validates
every row against the type schema, then writes a new version atomically.

get.summary:
`GET /api/v1/assets/{id}/usage` â€” **reverse lineage** (docs/20 Â§9): every run
(workflow instance) that pinned this asset, newest first. Answers "which runs
used asset X" straight from `workflow_instances.asset_pins` (GIN-indexed
jsonpath). Record/material-level lineage ("runs that used Copper C110") is a
deferred follow-on â€” see docs/20 Â§9.
GET /api/v1/auth/tokens â€” the caller's automation tokens.

post.summary: POST /api/v1/auth/tokens â€” mint a token. The `secret` is returned once.

404.description: Unknown token, or not the caller's

get.description:
List every registered backend with its display metadata, default editor
config, default output port shape, dispatch mode (executor job vs.
engine effect), resource channel (staged file vs. config overlay), and
schedulability.

The frontend reads this once per session (cached in
`app/src/lib/editor/backend-registry.svelte.ts`) and drives the
AutomatedStep editor panel from it â€” backend picker label/icon,
default config seed, default output port. The Svelte config-panel
component map (`backend-panels.ts`) stays hand-written; everything
else flows from here.

post.description:
Compute the canonical output [`Port`] for an AutomatedStep with this
backend, given its current config. Frontend calls this for
`output_authoring == "derived"` backends (LLM today) whenever the
step's config changes, so the read-only port editor always reflects
the actual runtime envelope.

Permissive: a half-typed config (missing fields, partial schema)
returns the closest valid port â€” not an error. Hard validation runs at
publish time via `BackendDecl::validate`.

Returns:
- 200 with the derived [`Port`] on success.
- 400 when the backend exists but isn't `Derived` (callers should use
  `default_output_port` from `GET /api/v1/backends` for `Fixed` /
  `Free` backends).
- 404 when the backend name doesn't resolve.

400.description: Backend's output is not derived from config

get.description:
List/search catalogue entries with full filter, sort, pagination support.

Query parameters:
  - `page`, `page_size` â€” pagination (0-based)
  - `sort` â€” e.g. `-created_at`, `+name`, `size_bytes`
  - `search` â€” free-text search across name, filename, storage_path
  - `filter[field][op]=value` â€” typed filters (eq, ne, gt, gte, lt, lte,
    contains, starts_with, ends_with, in, not_in, is_null, is_not_null)
  - `metadata` â€” JSONB containment on user_metadata (e.g. `{"kernel":"rbf"}`)
  - `file_metadata` â€” JSONB containment on file_metadata

Example:
  GET /api/v1/catalogue?filter[category][eq]=model&filter[source_net][contains]=surrogate&sort=-size_bytes&page=0&page_size=10

Distinct values for a JSONB key within file_metadata or user_metadata.
Example: GET /api/v1/catalogue/distinct-jsonb/file_metadata/format â†’ ["json", "csv"]

200.description: Distinct values for the JSONB key

get.description:
Returns distinct non-null values for a column (for populating filter dropdowns).
Column must be in the allowed filter fields whitelist.

Example: GET /api/v1/catalogue/distinct/category â†’ ["model", "dataset", "plot"]

get.summary: GET /api/v1/catalogue/download/{path} â€” download artifact bytes by storage path.
get.description:
The path parameter is the S3 storage_path from the catalogue entry.
Example: GET /api/v1/catalogue/download/artifacts/exec-123/gp_model/gp_model.json

get.summary: GET /api/v1/catalogue/lineage/{process_id} â€” all artifacts for a campaign, grouped by step.

get.description:
Aggregate statistics. Accepts the same filter params as the list endpoint,
so you can get stats for a subset (e.g., stats for a specific net or category).

List every REGISTERED datacenter (the `datacenter` resources in the DB),
overlaid with the engine's live `ClusterRegistry` state when a cluster
client is currently resident. This is the management view of "what clusters
exist", NOT "what clusters happen to hold a connection right now": the engine
builds a cluster client LAZILY on first lease and idle-tears-it-down after a
grace window, so a registered-but-idle datacenter has NO live engine entry.
Without the DB overlay it would vanish from the list the moment its last
lease drained â€” which is exactly the "my pools aren't visible" surprise.

A datacenter with no live client shows `watcher_state: "idle"` /
`connection_health: "idle"` and `active_lease_count: 0`. Any live engine
cluster NOT backed by a current DB row (e.g. the `_env` dev bootstrap, or a
just-deleted resource still draining) is appended so nothing is hidden.

Live-aggregated [`FleetMetrics`] over the `allocations` projection for the
caller's workspace: one [`ClusterMetrics`] per datacenter the workspace
touched in-window, plus a `fleet_total` rollup over the same row set. Counts
are over `datacenter_lease` rows with `acquired_at` inside the rolling
`[now - window, now]` window. NULL-`instance_id` pool-management nets are
excluded (not workspace-attributable). Default window `24h`.

parameters.name: window
parameters.description:
Lookback window: `Nd` / `Nh` / `Nm` (days / hours / minutes), e.g.
`24h`, `7d`, `90m`. Defaults to `24h`.

200.description: Per-cluster + fleet-total accounting over the window

post.description:
Gracefully drain a cluster: the engine refuses new leases for it, lets
in-flight leases finish, then idle-tears it down. Read-through of the
engine's `POST /api/clusters/{resource_id}/drain`.

get.description:
List the datacenter leases this cluster held, from the `allocations`
projection (`kind = 'datacenter_lease'`, filtered to this datacenter
resource). Distinct from `active_lease_count` on the live `ClusterSummary`
(an instantaneous gauge) â€” this is the historical ledger of leases against
the cluster, newest first, each with timing + accounting (`duration_ms`
computed: `released_at - acquired_at`, or live for a still-`held` lease).

`resource_id` is a datacenter resource UUID; the `_env` dev-bootstrap
cluster has no resource row and therefore no leases (returns `400` on an
unparseable id rather than silently emptying).

200.description: Datacenter leases held against this cluster

400.description: resource_id is not a valid datacenter UUID

get.description:
Live-aggregated [`ClusterMetrics`] over the `allocations` projection for a
single datacenter resource, scoped to the caller's workspace. Same window
semantics as the fleet endpoint. Returns an all-zero `ClusterMetrics` (not a
404) when the cluster has no in-window leases in the workspace.

parameters.name: window
parameters.description:
Lookback window: `Nd` / `Nh` / `Nm` (days / hours / minutes), e.g.
`24h`, `7d`, `90m`. Defaults to `24h`.

post.description:
Force-reconnect a cluster: the engine drops the watcher + allocator session
so the next fire rebuilds the client. Read-through of the engine's
`POST /api/clusters/{resource_id}/reconnect`.

Stateless compilation: accepts a graph (and optional inline file contents)
and returns AIR JSON without database access. Used by the editor's "Preview
AIR" button before publish.

post.summary:
POST /api/v1/files/upload/{id}/{node_id}
Accepts multipart/form-data with a `file` field.

201.description: File uploaded; returns S3 key + metadata

400.description: Invalid multipart or unsupported content type

get.summary:
GET /api/v1/files/{key}
Serves a file from S3 with the correct content type.
get.description:
`key` is a multi-segment S3 path (templates/{id}/v{ver}/{node}/{file}).
utoipa-axum 0.2 forwards the path string verbatim to axum::Router, so a
plain `{key}` only matches a single segment and every real key 404s before
reaching the handler. Use axum's catch-all `{*key}`; `Path<String>` still
extracts the full remaining path. (Same fix as the catalogue download
route â€” see commit d61bccb.)

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: S3 object key, may contain slashes (templates/{template_id}/v{ver}/{node_id}/{filename})

parameters.name: mode
parameters.description:
Filter by `mode`. Default behavior when omitted is to return only
`live` instances; pass `mode=any` to include drafts and test runs,
or `mode=draft` / `mode=test_run` to scope explicitly.

201.description: Instance created and deployed to engine

get.description:
Lists the resource grants (datacenter leases + token-pool admissions) this
instance held over its lifetime, from the `allocations` projection table.
Each row is one `(net_id, grant_id, kind)` grant: a LeaseScope / Loop body
holding a Slurm/Nomad/HTTP allocation (`datacenter_lease`), or an admission
against one of our own worker pools (`token_pool_grant`). The instance view
surfaces these to show "what did this run hold, for how long, and at what
cost" â€” `duration_ms` is computed (`released_at - acquired_at`, or live for
a still-`held` grant). Ordered by `acquired_at` (acquisition order).

200.description: Resource grants held by this instance

get.description:
Lists the sub-workflow child instances this instance spawned. A SubWorkflow
node runs its child as a separate engine net; the causality ingest
registers each spawn as a first-class child `workflow_instances` row
(parent_instance_id = this instance). A SubWorkflow inside a Loop/Map spawns
one child per iteration, so multiple rows can share `parent_node_id` â€”
ordered by `spawn_seq` (spawn/iteration order). The instance graph view
groups these by `parent_node_id` to offer an "Enter sub-workflow" drill-in
on each SubWorkflow node.

200.description: Sub-workflow child instances spawned by this instance

get.description: Returns the full event log for an instance from JetStream.

post.summary:
POST /api/v1/instances/{id}/promote-to-test â€” create a `template_tests` row
from an existing instance's event log.
post.description:
Scoops the instance's start tokens (from the initial Start-place tokens)
and human-task completions (from signal-place tokens) into a new test
fixture attached to the instance's template family. The user typically
completes authoring by adding assertions in the editor afterward.

409.description: A test with this name already exists

get.description:
Returns instance state with marking projected from JetStream events (source
of truth) and best-effort engine status for enabled transitions / run mode.

200.description: Instance state with marking + engine status

get.description:
Returns one row per workflow node Ã— execution iteration for an instance.
Materialized by the step-executions projection consumer; the frontend
overlays this data on the canvas node cards.

200.description: Per-step execution rows for this instance

get.description:
SSE stream of the instance's domain events, replayed from the start
(`DeliverPolicy::All`) then live, terminated by a final `result` event
carrying the structured envelope. Composes with FireAndForget: fire, get
the instance id, then open this stream. No per-instance ownership check â€”
consistent with `get_instance` (auth middleware gates the route).

200.description: SSE stream of domain events + final result

get.summary:
`GET /api/v1/job-templates` â€” paginated list, optionally filtered by flavor.
Returns the caller's workspace templates plus any public ones.

parameters.name: flavor
parameters.description: Optional filter: only return templates of this flavor (`slurm` | `nomad`).

parameters.name: workspace_id
parameters.description: Optional workspace filter. Defaults to the caller's workspace.

post.summary: `POST /api/v1/job-templates` â€” create a logical template + its v1 row.

get.summary: `GET /api/v1/job-templates/{id}` â€” detail view incl. versions + stagings.

put.summary:
`PUT /api/v1/job-templates/{id}` â€” update metadata and/or spec. Any of
`common_spec` / `escape_hatch` / `parameters` in the body bumps a new
version; metadata-only edits do not.

delete.summary:
`DELETE /api/v1/job-templates/{id}` â€” soft delete. Preserves version +
staging history.

post.summary:
`POST /api/v1/job-templates/{id}/stage` â€” push a template version onto one or
more datacenter clusters (B-staging, Phase 4). For each target this kicks a
generated staging Petri-net (`stage_template` effect) and upserts its
`template_stagings` row; the rows start at `staging` and the projection
advances them to `staged`/`failed` as the nets complete. Returns 202 with the
triggered rows.
post.description:
Authority = datacenter-resource access (workspace-scoped), not an admin role:
if you can reference cluster X in your workspace, you can stage to it. The
staging-net deploy is async, so this returns promptly.

Target selection: an explicit `datacenter_resource_ids` list fails the whole
request on the first incompatible target (a flavor mismatch is a user error).
With no list, it stages to EVERY workspace datacenter, silently skipping ones
whose flavor doesn't match the template (you only stage to compatible clusters).

get.summary: `GET /api/v1/job-templates/{id}/stagings` â€” list stagings for a template.

post.description:
Override the resolver's default workspace pick for this session. The
override rides on an HttpOnly companion cookie and survives until the
caller explicitly clears it (DELETE) or its membership is revoked.

Refuses workspaces the caller isn't a member of â€” a 403, not a silent
"did nothing" â€” so the picker UI can surface the error directly.

403.description: Not a member of the target workspace

delete.description:
Drop the override â€” the resolver's pick (or whatever the membership
default rule chooses on next login) takes over again. Idempotent: a
missing cookie still returns 204.

get.description:
List every registered workflow node type with its display metadata,
runtime kind, and protocol flags. The frontend reads this once per
session (cached alongside the backends registry) and drives the
editor's palette + property-panel dispatch from it. The Svelte
component map (`lib/components/editor/nodes/index.ts`) and the Lucide
icon map stay hand-written â€” components and icon imports can't be
serialized through JSON â€” but the palette label, description, kind,
and protocol flags flow from here.

Reads the dead-letter queue: every record any consumer ACKed and
dropped because it couldn't parse the input. Drains up to `limit`
recent records from `MEKHAN_SILENT_DROPS` and returns them along
with the process-wide counter.

The stream itself enforces retention (currently 7d / 10k msgs); this
endpoint just exposes whatever is currently retained.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Max records to return (default 100, hard cap 1000)

get.summary: GET /api/v1/processes â€” list processes with filter/sort/pagination.
get.description:
Query parameters use a custom DSL (see `query/extractor.rs`): `filter`,
`sort`, `page`, `page_size`. Response shape is paginated.

get.summary: GET /api/v1/processes/{process_id} â€” get process detail (with tasks, metrics, logs, artifact count).

200.description: Process detail with tasks, metrics, logs

put.summary: PUT /api/v1/processes/{process_id} â€” partial update of a process.

get.summary: GET /api/v1/processes/{process_id}/artifacts â€” list catalogue entries for a process.
SSE: emits `artifact` events whose `data` field is a JSON-stringified `LiveArtifactEvent`.
GET /api/v1/processes/{process_id}/logs â€” list logs for a process with filter/pagination.
SSE: emits `log` events whose `data` field is a JSON-stringified `LiveLogEvent`.

parameters.name: execution_id
parameters.description:
Narrow to a single executor execution. Each executor job maps 1:1 to a
workflow step+iteration, so this is the reliable key for scoping logs to
a particular step in the instance view (the `StepExecution` row has no
execution_id of its own â€” it lives on the parked output envelope and on
every log line's `detail.fields.execution_id`, stamped by the executor's
`enrich_log_fields`). Matched against `detail.fields.execution_id` with a
fallback to a top-level `detail.execution_id` to mirror the frontend's
historical client-side filter.

get.summary: GET /api/v1/processes/{process_id}/metrics â€” list metrics for a process.

200.description: Backfilled metric series (adaptive bucket when window > max_points)

get.summary:
SSE: emits `connected`, optional `gap`/`resync`, then `metric` events
whose `data` field is a JSON-stringified `LiveMetricEvent`.
GET /api/v1/processes/{process_id}/metrics/summary â€” aggregated metric stats per key.
GET /api/v1/processes/{process_id}/tasks â€” list tasks for a process.

patch.description:
Rename / re-describe a project. `slug` is immutable (it's the stable
filter key the templates list and OpenAPI bundle route hang off of), so
only `display_name` and `description` are mutable. Omitted fields are
left untouched via COALESCE.

post.description:
Attach a template (by *base* id â€” the chain root). The caller must be
an editor on the project's workspace AND able to read the template
(workspace member OR template is public).

get.description:
Resolves a catalogue entry to its producing token and returns the full
ancestry chain. Uses `source_event_sequence` for direct lookup when
available, falling back to signal_key â†’ cross-link resolution.

parameters.name: execution_id
parameters.description: Execution id from the catalogue entry

200.description: Ancestry walked back from the producing token

get.description: Look up a cross-net bridge link by signal_key.

Returns the full context for a causality event by joining to domain
tables based on effect_handler. Enables rich detail views in the
provenance DAG visualization.

parameters.name: event_seq
parameters.description: Event sequence number within the net

200.description: Full event detail with related rows

get.description:
Recursive CTE walking token ancestry: for a given token, find which events
produced it, what tokens those events consumed, and recurse.

parameters.name: token_id
parameters.description: Token id to walk ancestry from

get.summary: `GET /api/v1/resources` â€” paginated list, optionally filtered by type.

parameters.name: resource_type
parameters.description: Optional filter: only return resources of this type.

parameters.name: workspace_id
parameters.description:
Optional workspace filter. v1 default is `Uuid::nil()` so the
no-workspace deployment Just Works; when workspaces land, the auth
layer fills this in.

parameters.name: scope
parameters.description:
Scope context for downward-visibility resolution (docs/20 Â§2). Format:
`workspace`, `project:<uuid>`, or `template:<uuid>`. When present it
overrides `workspace_id`: the list returns the most-specific-wins
visible set for the binding context. When absent, the legacy flat
`workspace_id` filter applies.

parameters.name: folder
parameters.description: Optional virtual-folder prefix filter on `display_path` (docs/20 Â§3).

post.summary: `POST /api/v1/resources` â€” create a logical resource and its v1 row.

get.summary:
`GET /api/v1/resources/types` â€” registry introspection. Powers the
frontend picker's type list and the schema-driven create form.

`GET /api/v1/resources/{id}` â€” admin view. Secret fields are listed by
name only; values never leave Vault on the read path.

put.summary:
`PUT /api/v1/resources/{id}` â€” update display_name and/or config. Setting
`config` bumps `latest_version` and writes a fresh vault_path; name-only
updates do not.

delete.summary:
`DELETE /api/v1/resources/{id}` â€” soft delete. Preserves
`resource_versions` rows + Vault paths so already-pinned instances keep
resolving.

get.summary: `GET /api/v1/resources/{id}/audit` â€” paginated audit trail for a resource.

post.summary:
`POST /api/v1/resources/{id}/rotate` â€” write a new version. Identical to
`update_resource` with only `config` set, plus a different audit verb.

200.description: Resource rotated to a new version

get.summary: GET /api/v1/tasks â€” list all tasks with filter/sort/pagination.
get.description:
Returns `{ tasks, total, page, page_size, total_pages, has_next, has_previous }`
where each task is a `HumanTask`-shaped JSON object (see `to_human_task_json`).
The `tasks` key is what the Mekhan frontend's task store expects; the rest
of the pagination envelope is preserved for richer clients.

get.summary: GET /api/v1/tasks/stream â€” SSE endpoint for real-time task events from NATS.
get.description:
Emits `{event, data}` lines for `task_created`, `task_completed`,
`task_failed`, `task_cancelled`, `process_update`. `data` is the raw
NATS payload as a JSON string (clients re-parse it).

200.description: SSE stream of task lifecycle events

get.summary: GET /api/v1/tasks/:id â€” get a single task.
get.description:
Returns a `HumanTask`-shaped JSON object built from the DB row + `detail`
JSONB projected by the causality consumer. This includes `task_id`, `steps`,
`instructions_mdsvex`, `net_id`, `place`, etc. â€” everything the frontend
task form needs to render.

post.summary: POST /api/v1/tasks/{id}/cancel â€” cancel a task and publish NATS signal.
POST /api/v1/tasks/{id}/complete â€” complete a task and publish NATS signal.

requestBody.description: Completion payload â€” `data` field is forwarded as the task result

get.description:
Latest-version catalogue listing driven by the generic list DSL:
  - `page`, `page_size` â€” pagination (0-based)
  - `sort` â€” e.g. `-updated_at`, `name`, `version`
  - `search` â€” free-text across name + description
  - `filter[field][op]=value` â€” typed filters over direct columns
    (published, version, visibility, created_at, â€¦)

plus the template-specific relational/security params in
[`TemplateListExtras`] (`project_id`, `tag`, `base_template_id`,
`owner_template_id`).

parameters.name: base_template_id
parameters.description:
Version-chain mode: list every version of this base family (ignoring
`is_latest`) instead of the default latest-only catalogue listing.

parameters.name: project_id
parameters.description:
Restrict to templates attached to a project (M:N via
`project_templates.base_template_id`). The join is non-restrictive
w.r.t. version chain â€” the live `is_latest` row wins.

parameters.name: tag
parameters.description: Restrict to templates carrying this tag in the user's workspace.

parameters.name: owner_template_id
parameters.description:
Enumerate the private sub-workflow children owned by this parent
family (`COALESCE(base_template_id, id)`). When supplied, the listing
returns *only* those private children (they're otherwise hidden from
the catalogue). When absent, private templates are excluded entirely.

post.description:
Clinic-style headless template upload: accepts pre-compiled AIR
(`ScenarioDefinition` shape â€” `{places[], transitions[]}`) directly,
bypassing the editor's `WorkflowGraph` â†’ AIR compile pass entirely.
The supplied `air_json` is stored verbatim into the `air_json` column;
a synthetic stub graph containing just the `Trigger` node is stored
into the `graph` column so the trigger dispatcher's `register_triggers`
finds it post-commit.

Idempotency: name-based, scoped per workspace. A first apply with a
given `name` in the caller's workspace Seeds a fresh v1 chain
(`is_latest = true`); subsequent applies with the same `(name,
workspace_id)` pair Bump the chain. Cross-workspace name collisions
are independent chains.

Distinct from `POST /api/v1/templates/{id}/apply` (the GitOps path for
graph-authored templates): that one demands an existing `{id}` and a
`WorkflowGraph`, then runs the compile pass. This endpoint takes
neither.

200.description: Applied: seeded v1 or a new born-published version

delete.summary:
DELETE /api/v1/templates/{id}
Per Section 11.7: cascade cleanup for published templates with finished instances.

200.description: Compiled AIR JSON for the published template

post.description:
GitOps entry point: atomically publish a new version of the chain straight
from a git-authored artifact. The supplied `graph` REPLACES the chain head
wholesale (no CRDT merge). Either seeds-and-publishes a fresh `mekhan init`
draft as v1 in place, or bumps the published head to a new born-published
version. The collaborative Y.Doc draft window is collapsed to nothing, so
publish-freeze itself is the isolation between git- and web-authored
templates.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Any template id in the target chain

200.description: Applied: seeded v1 or a new born-published version

409.description: Chain head is an unpublished web-editor draft

Compiled AIR JSON preview from current draft graph

get.description:
Resolve a child template family (by base id or any version-row id) per the
optional `version` pin â€” the SAME resolution `resolve_subworkflow_air` uses
at publish â€” and return its derived SubWorkflow contract. The editor's
preview therefore cannot drift from the contract frozen into the parent.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Child template family id (base or any version row)

parameters.name: version
parameters.description:
Pin to a specific child version. Omit for the family's latest published
row (matches a SubWorkflow node's `versionPin: latest`).

get.summary:
GET /api/v1/templates/{id}/io-stubs
Generated `_aithericon_io` pair (`.py` SDK delegate + typed `.pyi` overlay)
per Python automated step, derived from each node's input scope. An
authoring aid: unlike compile it does NOT require the graph to be
publishable (missing entrypoints / dangling edges are fine) â€” it only needs
a DAG. The IDE surfaces these read-only so step code gets typed
`load_input()` before publish. Non-fatal by design: a graph that can't be
scoped yields an empty map, never an error, so the editor never breaks on
this.

get.description:
Resolve any id in a template's version chain to the row currently flagged
`is_latest`. Accepts the chain root (`base_template_id`) â€” the stable
identifier the CLI's `mekhan.lock.json` pins â€” or any historical version
id; both resolve through the same `base_template_id` column.

CLI commands that need "the chain head right now" (`run`, `test`, the
post-pull bundle fetch) call this first, then operate on the returned id.
The split keeps `/bundle`, `/instances`, `/tests/...` semantics
strictly version-id-scoped (you can still pull a historical version by
passing its concrete id) â€” the resolver layer is the only place that
follows the chain.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Any template id in the version chain (base or a specific version)

200.description: The latest version in the template's chain

201.description: New draft version created from published source

get.description:
Projects this template (by chain root) is currently attached to, within
its workspace. Read-gated like the tags endpoint so the assign dialog can
show membership and offer a detach toggle without a fan-out.

parameters.name: force
parameters.description:
Bypass the template-test gate. Failing or stale tests do not block
publish when `true`; an audit-level log records the override. Use
only when a test itself is broken and you need to ship.

200.description: Template published; AIR compiled and stored

get.description:
Tags currently on this template's version chain. Read-gated (viewer or
public): populates the tag editor on the template detail page so a full
replace via PUT starts from the existing set rather than clobbering it.

get.summary: GET /api/v1/templates/{id}/tests â€” list tests for a template family.

post.summary: POST /api/v1/templates/{id}/tests â€” create a new test.

POST /api/v1/templates/{id}/tests/run-all â€” run every enabled test for a
template family. Used by the editor's "Run all" button and by the

parameters.description:
Run only enabled tests (default). `?include_disabled=true` runs every

parameters.name: id

patch.description:
`public` is cross-workspace exposure â€” a tenancy decision, so it requires
admin. `workspace` and `private` are authoring-scope changes (a private
sub-workflow is bound to its parent, never exposed beyond the workspace),
so an editor building workflows can set them.

403.description: Insufficient role (admin for public, editor otherwise)

get.description:
Returns newest-first. The editor's detail sheet shows the latest one
(failure_detail + final_scope + instance_id) so authors can diagnose a
failure without chasing it through `/instances`.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Cap on returned rows. Defaults to 10; ignored if non-positive.

get.description:
Returns aggregate counters per source kind plus the registry size. Useful
for /admin dashboards and the editor's trigger landing page.

post.description:
Returns the next N fire times for a cron schedule. Used by the editor's
trigger inspector to show users when their cron will fire next without
having to ship the workflow first.

get.description:
The per-source scope contract, surfaced so the editor can show authors
exactly which identifiers are in scope under each mapping expression
instead of leaving them to guess.

200.description: Available scope identifiers for the source kind

patch.description:
Arm or pause a single trigger on its **published** template. This is the
deliberate inverse of the rest of the template: a trigger's `source`,
`payload_mapping` and target are frozen at publish, but whether it is
currently armed is operational state of the live template â€” so it is
editable exactly when everything else is locked, and *only* then (drafts
are armed by editing the graph in the editor).

The published row's `graph` column is the runtime source of truth that
`hydrate()` and `fire()` read, so we flip `enabled` on the trigger node
there and re-register the template to make the change live immediately;
it then survives restarts via the normal `hydrate()` path.

post.description:
Async, fire-and-forget. Parses the body (JSON or multipart â€” see
[`parse_fire_body`]), fires the trigger, and returns immediately without
waiting for the spawned instance. A `Spawned` outcome returns
`202 { instance_id }`; a signal-kind / dropped outcome returns the
`FireResult` JSON with 200. To consume the run's events, stream
`GET /api/v1/instances/{id}/stream`; to wait for a terminal result inline,
use the sibling `.../invoke` route.

200.description: Signal-kind / dropped fire â€” no instance spawned.

202.description: Instance spawned â€” fire-and-forget. Body is `{ instance_id }`.

400.description: Fire failed (e.g. mapping or instance error)

post.description:
Sync (WaitForResult): parses the body identically to `.../fire`, then holds
the HTTP connection until the spawned instance reaches a terminal state and
returns its result envelope (`200`, `FireTriggerResponse` with `outcome`).
Bounded by `wait_timeout_secs`; on timeout the waiter is deregistered and
the response degrades to `202 { instance_id }` so the caller can poll or
stream. A signal-kind / dropped fire has no instance to wait on and returns
the `FireResult` shape with 200.

200.description: Instance reached a terminal state â€” body carries the result envelope + `outcome`.

202.description: Wait timed out â€” instance still running; poll/stream it. Body is `{ instance_id }`.

400.description: Fire failed (e.g. mapping or instance error)

post.description:
Resolves an email address to the OIDC subject used by Mekhan as the
principal id. Authenticated (any role) â€” Zitadel does its own ACL on
the broker PAT; in dev_noop the response is computed locally without
touching any directory.

get.description:
Lists every workspace the caller is a member of. Authenticated; no
per-workspace gate (the caller is implicitly restricted by their
`workspace_members` rows).

post.description:
Adds a member identified by OIDC `subject`. Server derives `user_id`
via `uuid_v5(SUBJECT_UUID_NAMESPACE, subject)` so this works for
principals that haven't yet logged into mekhan. Upserts so calling
twice with a different role flips the role rather than failing.

delete.description:
Removes a member. Refuses to remove the last `owner` so the workspace
can't be orphaned.

get.description:
Distinct tags across every template in the workspace. Drives the tag
filter chips in the templates list â€” one round trip, no per-template
fan-out.

Returns a synthesized OpenAPI 3.0.3 document covering every callable
trigger in the project's attached templates. The shape is suitable for
feeding into `openapi-typescript`, `openapi-generator`, or any OAS3
viewer.

404.description: Project not found or not in workspace

get.description:
Liveness probe â€” fixed shape, no DB / NATS dependencies. Lives at the
root (NOT under `/api/v1`) and OUTSIDE the auth layer so load balancers,
Nomad/k8s probes, and uptime monitors can poll it without a session
cookie. The path follows k8s convention; spec stays in the OpenAPI doc
for completeness but the runtime mount is intentionally separate from
the main protected `OpenApiRouter`.

subject.description:
OIDC `sub` claim â€” the server derives `user_id` via
`uuid_v5(SUBJECT_UUID_NAMESPACE, subject)`. Phase B will add an
emailâ†’subject resolver for the admin UI.

AllocationResponse.description:
Response shape for `GET /api/v1/instances/{id}/allocations` and
`GET /api/v1/clusters/{id}/leases`.

One row of the `allocations` projection table â€” a resource grant on the
Petri substrate: either a `datacenter_lease` (an external-cluster
Slurm/Nomad/HTTP allocation held by a LeaseScope / Loop body) or a
`token_pool_grant` (an admission against one of our own worker pools).
Materialized field-for-field by the allocations projection consumer
(sequence-guarded upsert keyed on `(net_id, grant_id, kind)`), with a
computed `duration_ms` overlaid the same way `StepExecutionResponse` does.

Every nullable column mirrors a `NULL`-able DB column: pool grants carry no
`cluster_resource_id` / `scheduler_flavor`; pool-management nets carry no
`instance_id`; timing/accounting fields fill in as the grant progresses.

alloc_id.description: Slurm jobid / Nomad dispatched job id.

cpu_seconds.description: Rounded CPU-seconds (the engine payload float is rounded to `i64`).

duration_ms.description:
`released_at - acquired_at`, or `now - acquired_at` while `held`.
`None` until the grant is acquired. Computed by the handler â€” not a
column, so it defaults to `None` when read via `FromRow`.

grant_id.description: Engine grant key (`instance_id:node_id`); equals the accounting signal key.

instance_id.description: Resolved owning instance; `None` for pool-management nets.

node_id.description: Workflow node / LeaseScope container id that holds the grant.

ApplyAirTemplateRequest.description:
Request body for `POST /api/templates/apply-air` â€” clinic-style
headless template upload.

Accepts pre-compiled AIR directly: no `WorkflowGraph` compile pass,
no Y.Doc init, no S3 file upload. The supplied `air_json` is stored
verbatim into the `air_json` column; a synthetic stub graph (one
Trigger node, no edges) is stored into the `graph` column so the
trigger dispatcher's `register_triggers` finds it.

Idempotency: name-based. Re-apply with the same `name` Bumps the
chain (new version row, prior version's triggers forgotten); first
apply Seeds (fresh chain at v1).

air_json.description:
Pre-compiled AIR. Stored verbatim. The endpoint runs no compile
pass; the AIR is consumed by the engine at trigger-fire time.

oneOf.description:
Optional git provenance, recorded into `source_ref` exactly like
the existing GitOps `apply` endpoint.

ApplyTemplateRequest.description:
Request body for `POST /api/v1/templates/{id}/apply` â€” the GitOps path.
The `graph` REPLACES the chain head wholesale (no CRDT merge); binary
assets are uploaded out-of-band via the files endpoint before this call.

ArtifactsListResponse.description:
Response shape for `GET /api/v1/processes/{process_id}/artifacts/list`.

Frontend reads `body.entries[]` directly â€” keep the single-field envelope.

Assertion.description:
A single check evaluated against the runner's synthetic post-instance scope
(`{ result, steps.<slug>.output }`). `path` is a dot-pathed JSON pointer
into that scope (e.g. `"result.value.invoice_amount"`,
`"steps.review.output.approved"`). Evaluation is intentionally data-driven â€”
no DSL parser. See `handlers::template_tests::runner::eval_assertion`.

value.description:
Right-hand side. For `Exists`/`NotExists` this is ignored; for
`Matches` it must be a string (regex); for `Contains` it can be a
scalar (substring on string actual / membership on array actual).

AssetBinding.description:
A node-level asset binding (docs/20 Â§5). Analogous to `resource_alias`:
the author picks an asset by its scope-resolved `ref_key`, and the compiler
stages the asset's whole record collection as an ordinary input the node
reads under `alias` (`<alias>.json`). Business data never enters the control
token â€” the records ride the same staging machinery as file inputs.

Whole-collection granularity only in v1 (the node does its own lookup in
code). Author-picked-row / runtime-filter are deferred (docs/20 Â§9).

alias.description:
The staged-input name the node code reads (`<alias>.json`). Must be a
flat identifier so it doesn't collide with a producer slug / resource
name / control-token field. Defaults to `ref_key` when the author
doesn't override it.

refKey.description:
The asset's scope-resolved flat ref-key (`steel`, `materials_db`).
Resolved at publish time through the scope resolver to a stable
`(asset_id, version)` pin baked into the AIR.

AssetDetail.description: Full view for `GET /api/v1/assets/{id}` â€” metadata + a page of records.

record_count.description: Total record count for the current version (for pagination).

records.description: The current-version records (paged). Each is a validated JSONB row.

AssetFileUpload.description: Spec-only multipart wrapper for an asset File-field upload.

AssetFileUploadResponse.description:
Response of `POST /api/v1/assets/{id}/files` â€” the storage path to drop into
a record's `File` field value.

AssetTypeDetail.description: Full view for `GET /api/v1/asset-types/{id}` â€” carries the schema.

fields.description: The schema: an ordered list of [`PortField`]s.

AssetTypeSummary.description:
Compact list-row for `GET /api/v1/asset-types` â€” omits `fields_json` so the
list stays cheap.

AssetUsageItem.description:
One run (workflow instance) that pinned a given asset â€” a reverse-lineage
row for `GET /api/v1/assets/{id}/usage` (docs/20 Â§9). `alias` /
`version_used` are extracted from the instance's `asset_pins` map for the
queried asset. This is **asset-level** lineage ("runs that used asset X");
record/material-level lineage ("runs that used Copper C110") is deferred â€”
see docs/20 Â§9.

alias.description: The binding alias under which this run consumed the asset.

version_used.description: The asset version this run pinned (immutable for the life of the run).

template_id.description:
The *base* template id (the first version's id, which the
`is_latest`-chained version graph hangs off of). Project membership
follows the live version chain so attaching once survives version
bumps.

AttachmentSpec.description:
One attachment. The compiler emits one of these per `attachments[]` entry
in the workflow config and pairs it with a synthesized `inputs[]` entry
that materializes the file into the run's staged-inputs directory.

filename.description: Filename the recipient sees in the mail client.

input_name.description: Staged-input name where the bytes live (one of `RunContext.staged_inputs`).

mime.description: Optional MIME override; otherwise inferred from `filename` extension.

oneOf.description: Bearer token: `Authorization: Bearer <token>`
Basic auth: `Authorization: Basic base64(user:pass)`
Custom header-based auth (e.g., `X-API-Key: <value>`)

BackendDescriptor.description:
Frontend-visible metadata for one backend. Returned by `GET /api/v1/backends`.

The Svelte component map (`backend-panels.ts`) stays hand-written â€” TS
can't import components dynamically from a JSON tag at runtime without
defeating Vite chunking â€” but every other per-backend constant
(display name, icon, default config, default output fields, dispatch
mode, resource channel) flows from here. This is what kills the
`automated-ports.ts` â†” `default_output_port()` drift hazard.

configSchema.description:
Self-contained JSON Schema for this backend's `spec.config` shape
(`#/components/schemas/*` refs inlined). The SPA's generic
schema-driven form renders simple panels off this. `null` for
backends with no executor config type.

consumesDeclaredOutputs.description:
Whether this backend's declared output port fields drive a Rhai
`outputs:` constant (mostly informational for the frontend).

defaultEditorConfig.description:
Seed config inserted into a fresh step when this backend is
selected. Opaque JSON â€” the backend's Svelte config panel decodes
its own structure.

defaultOutputPort.description:
Canonical output port shape. Frontend uses this for the "Reset to
default" button on the output port editor.

name.description:
Snake-case wire tag (`"smtp"`, `"python"`, â€¦). Matches
[`ExecutionBackendType`]'s wire encoding and the executor's
`ExecutionSpec.backend` string.

outputAuthoring.description:
Who owns the output port shape â€” user (free), backend (fixed), or
derived from config. Drives the editor's port-section rendering.

schedulable.description: Whether the editor should show the Scheduled deployment toggle.

secretFields.description:
`config` field names that hold secrets (rendered masked by the
generic form).

userAuthorable.description:
Whether this backend can be selected as an AutomatedStep backend in
the node-authoring picker. `GET /api/v1/backends` only returns
authorable backends; this field is carried for frontend transparency.

BackoffKind.description:
Delay applied between automated-step retry attempts.

`Immediate` re-dispatches at once. `Fixed` waits `base_delay_ms` before
every attempt. `Exponential` waits `base_delay_ms * 2^attempt` (attempt is
the zero-based retry index), capped by the engine's timer service.

CalloutSeverity.description:
Severity for callout blocks. Snake-case on the wire (`"info"`,
`"warning"`, `"error"`, `"success"`) to keep the byte-for-byte shape that
the editor and human-task UI already produce/consume.

backfill.description:
If true, the dispatcher walks existing catalogue entries matching the
filters when the trigger is first registered.

CatalogueEntry.description: A single catalogue entry (maps 1:1 to the `catalogue_entries` table).

ChatMessage.description:
A single message in conversation history.

`content` is a JSON value, not a bare string, because tool-result
messages (`role: tool`) carry the structured tool output directly; the
adapters render it to the string each provider's wire format expects.
Text turns (system/user/assistant) carry a JSON string.

tool_call_id.description: For `role: tool` â€” the id of the assistant tool call this answers.

tool_calls.description: For `role: assistant` â€” the tool calls the model emitted this turn.

applied.description:
`true` when the cluster was live and the action applied; `false` when no
such cluster is currently resident (a no-op).

ClusterMetrics.description:
Live-aggregated accounting for one cluster (or the fleet rollup) over a
rolling time window, computed straight off the `allocations` projection.

All counts/sums are over `datacenter_lease` rows whose `acquired_at` falls
inside `[window_start, window_end]` AND whose owning instance resolves into
the requesting user's workspace (NULL-`instance_id` pool-management nets are
excluded â€” they are not workspace-attributable). The percentiles are
`PERCENTILE_CONT` over `queue_wait_ms` of the acquired rows; the `held_*`
gauges are an instantaneous read of the still-held leases (`released_at IS
NULL`), decoded best-effort from `requested_tres`.

active_lease_count.description: Currently-held leases (`released_at IS NULL`) â€” instantaneous gauge.

cluster_id.description: Datacenter `resource_id` (UUID string), or `"fleet"` for the rollup.

held_cpu_seconds.description: Î£ requested CPU-count of currently-held leases (best-effort, NULL-safe).

held_gpu_seconds.description: Î£ requested GPU-count of currently-held leases (best-effort, NULL-safe).

resource_path.description:
The datacenter resource's snake_case `path`, when it resolves. `None`
for the fleet rollup or a deleted resource.

ClusterSummary.description:
One live cluster's observable state, as surfaced to the control plane.

Mirrors the engine's `ClusterView` payload, enriched with the human name of
the backing datacenter resource (`path` / `display_name`) when it resolves.

active_lease_count.description: Held leases + in-flight submits referencing this cluster.

cursor.description: Last checkpoint cursor (poll timestamp / Nomad event index), if recorded.

display_name.description: The datacenter resource's human display name, when it resolves.

draining.description: Whether this cluster is draining (refusing new leases).

last_signal_at.description: RFC3339 timestamp of the most recent signal delivery, if any.

resource_id.description:
The datacenter `resource_id` (a UUID), or `"_env"` for the single
env-driven dev-bootstrap cluster.

resource_path.description:
The datacenter resource's snake_case `path` (e.g. `prod_slurm`), when the
`resource_id` resolves to a row. `None` for `_env` / deleted resources.

version.description: The datacenter resource version this client was built from.

CommonSpec.description:
Typed flavor-neutral core of a job template version. Every field is optional
â€” a template may specify as much or as little as it likes; the flavor's
staging step fills in defaults. Serialized into `job_template_versions.common_spec`.

time_limit.description: Walltime string in the flavor's own grammar (e.g. Slurm `"01:30:00"`).

CompileErrorView.description:
Structured payload of a compile error for the editor. Returned as part of
the publish API response so the frontend can highlight the offending
node/edge inline instead of just showing a flat error string.

CompileRequest.description:
Request body for stateless compilation. Used by `POST /api/v1/compile` and
`POST /api/v1/templates/{id}/compile`. `files` is a per-node, per-filename map
of inline contents; the preview compile emits `InputSource::Raw` entries so
the AIR matches the StoragePath-keyed shape produced by publish.

template_id.description:
Template the draft belongs to. When present, `/api/v1/analyze` resolves
template-visible **assets** referenced by the graph (`<asset>.<field>`)
into the same "Globals" scope.

workspace_id.description:
Workspace the draft belongs to. When present, `POST /api/v1/analyze`
resolves workspace-scoped **resources** referenced by the graph so the
editor picker / diagnostics see resource public fields (`<resource>.<field>`)
as a known "Globals" scope instead of a false unresolved. Absent on the
stateless `/api/v1/compile` path (which has no DB context).

Compression.description: Compression algorithm for streaming copy/move transfers.

oneOf.description: Every fire produces an event (default).
At most one fire in flight; subsequent fires are dropped while running.
Queue fires behind the active one; drained when it completes.

Dedup by hashing the result of a Rhai `expression` over the event scope;
fires whose key has been seen within `window_secs` are dropped.

dedup_key.description:
Dedup by hashing the result of a Rhai `expression` over the event scope;
fires whose key has been seen within `window_secs` are dropped.

ContextStrategy.description:
Context-window management strategy for an [`WorkflowNodeData::Agent`].
Inert in PR 1's degenerate path; declared upfront so the type stays
stable across the follow-up loop-lowering PR (`docs/12` Â§ 3).

CreateAssetTypeRequest.description:
Request body for `POST /api/v1/asset-types`. Validates the schema +
ident-grammar `name`.

fields.description: The schema â€” an ordered list of [`PortField`]s.

scope_id.description: Owner scope id. For `workspace`, defaults to the caller's workspace.

oneOf.description: Owner scope. Defaults to `workspace` of the caller when omitted.

metadata.description:
Free-form audit metadata stored on the instance row. Unlike pre-typed-ports
behavior, this is NOT merged into initial Petri tokens â€” token shape is
driven solely by `start_tokens`.

mode.description:
`live` (default) | `draft` | `test_run`. `test_run` is reserved for
the test runner â€” callers requesting it directly are rejected.

start_tokens.description:
Typed seeds for each Start block in the template. A Start with a
non-empty `initial` port requires a matching entry here; otherwise the
API returns 400. Starts with an empty `initial` port can be omitted
(each gets a default `{}` token with system fields injected).

CreateJobTemplateRequest.description:
Request body for `POST /api/v1/job-templates`. Lands a `job_templates` row
at `latest_version = 1` plus the first `job_template_versions` row (v1).

slug.description: Identifier-safe key, unique within a workspace.

workspace_id.description: Optional workspace scoping. `None` resolves to the caller's workspace.

CreateResourceRequest.description:
Request body for `POST /api/v1/resources`. Carries every field needed to
land both a `resources` row and the first `resource_versions` row.

config.description:
Full config map â€” both public and secret fields. The handler splits
it by descriptor lists.

display_name.description: UI label. Defaults to `path` if empty.

workspace_id.description:
Optional workspace scoping. No `workspaces` table exists in v1; a
`None` here resolves to `Uuid::nil()`. When the table lands, this
will be set by the auth layer.

files.description:
Optional per-node file map (filename â†’ inline contents). Files are
seeded as Y.Text entries inside each node's `files` Y.Map so that the
new template lands ready-to-publish for backends that require
attached scripts (e.g. Python's entrypoint).

human_answers.description:
Map of `<node_slug>` â†’ form data captured by the human-task UI.
Missing slugs fail the run at the first un-stubbed `human.request`.

description.description: Optional longer note â€” stored as the machine-user `description`.

expires_at.description: Optional RFC 3339 expiry for the underlying PAT. Omit for no expiry.

name.description:
Human-friendly label â€” stored as the backing Zitadel machine-user
`name`, shown in the token list.

CreatedToken.description:
Response of `POST /api/v1/auth/tokens`. Identical to [`TokenSummary`] plus the
one-time `secret` â€” Mekhan never stores or re-serves it.

secret.description: The Personal Access Token. Present only here, only once.

count.description: Number of upcoming fire times to compute (clamped to 1..=10).

error.description:
Error message if the schedule or timezone is invalid; `upcoming` is
empty when set.

catchup.description: What to do after a service restart with missed fire windows.

jitterSecs.description:
Optional jitter window in seconds; the dispatcher fires within
`[scheduled, scheduled + jitter_secs]` to spread load.

schedule.description: Standard cron expression (5- or 6-field). Validated at compile time.

timezone.description: IANA timezone (e.g. `"Europe/Berlin"`). Defaults to `"UTC"` if absent.

CrossNetEdge.description: Explicit cross-net edge resolved from causality_cross_links.

CsvImportBody.description: Spec-only multipart wrapper for the CSV import body.

DemoResetReport.description:
Tally of a [`purge_seeded`] / [`reseed_all`] run. Serializable so the admin
HTTP endpoint hands it straight back to the operator / CLI.

familiesRemoved.description: Distinct seeded template *families* (by base id) deleted.

instancesPurged.description:
Workflow instances (across every version of those families) whose
engine nets were purged and DB rows deleted.

seeded.description: Demos freshly re-seeded. Only ever non-zero from [`reseed_all`].

testsRemoved.description: Bundled template-test rows removed alongside the families.

oneOf.description:
Dispatch to our executor daemon pool over the NATS work queue (jobs are
pulled by the long-running executor workers â€” NOT in-process). `pool:
None` is the plain path: our worker pool is currently unbounded (no
control plane gating concurrency yet), so a job runs as soon as a worker
is free. `pool: Some` adds BOUNDED admission on top â€” a `token_pool`
claim/register/release handshake so contended infrastructure (GPUs, lab
machines, LLM slots) is admission-controlled by the Petri firing rule
(R3). The bound alias MUST be a `token_pool` resource â€” a `datacenter`
belongs under [`DeploymentModel::Scheduled`].

Lease through an external cluster. `scheduler` names a `datacenter`
resource (docs/13). `job_template` selects the scheduler's parameterized
job (e.g. `petri-mumax3-worker`).

jobTemplate.description:
Legacy/manual native job NAME registered on the scheduler. When
`job_template_ref` is `Some`, publish OVERWRITES this string with the
referenced template's slug (the name Phase-4 staging registers the
native job under), so lowering/engine always read a concrete name
here regardless of which authoring path produced it.

oneOf.description:
Optional control-plane job-template REFERENCE (Phase 3, B-model).
When `Some`, publish resolves+validates it against the step's
resolved cluster (`resolve_job_templates`) and stamps the template's
slug into `job_template`. `None` â‡’ the bare `job_template` string is
used verbatim (legacy/manual path). The actual staging mechanism is
Phase 4 â€” this field only drives resolve+validate at publish.

DeploymentModel.description:
Where an `AutomatedStep`'s job runs. Internally tagged on the wire by
`mode`: `{"mode":"executor", ...}` or `{"mode":"scheduled", ...}`. Keep the
`mode` strings in lockstep with the `snake_case` derive.

`executor` vs `scheduled` is the physically-honest split: our own executor
daemon pool (jobs dispatched over the NATS work queue and pulled by the
long-running executor workers) vs an external cluster. Resource admission
*is* scheduling, so:
- a `token_pool` admission lives under [`DeploymentModel::Executor`]'s `pool`
  (the body runs on our executor pool holding the typed lease â€” R1â€“R3
  machinery), and
- an external cluster is a `datacenter` resource bound under
  [`DeploymentModel::Scheduled`]'s `scheduler` (docs/13), with `operation`
  selecting submit (today's sbatch/dispatch) vs lease (R4).

oneOf.description:
Standard executor dispatch. The compiler emits an executor job; the
step's `DeploymentModel` decides whether it's inline via NATS or
dispatched to an external cluster.

Engine builtin effect (e.g. CatalogueQuery â†’ `catalogue_lookup`). The
compiler skips executor lowering entirely and emits an effect handler
invocation directly into the Petri transition.

DispatchMode.description:
Lowering mode â€” intrinsic to the backend, decided at the decl, NOT the
step. Orthogonal to `DeploymentModel` (Inline / Scheduled) which is a
per-step author choice on any `ExecutorJob` backend.

DockerConfig.description: Configuration for the Docker execution backend.

command.description: Command to run in the container (Docker CMD).

env.description: Environment variables to set in the container.

oneOf.description: Image pull policy.

remove_container.description: Remove the container after execution (equivalent to --rm).

oneOf.description: Optional resource limits for the container.

DownloadItemConfig.description:
One entry in a `download` task block. Mirrors the frontend `DownloadItem`
(`app/src/lib/hpi/types.ts`) field-for-field on the wire.

ErrorResponse.description:
Uniform error body returned by every fallible handler. The spec exposes a
single `ErrorResponse` schema and the frontend gets one consistent error
shape: switch on `code` (machine-readable, stable kebab-case) for
programmatic handling; render `error` for humans.

`code` is populated automatically by every `ApiError::*` constructor.
Stable values: `"bad-request"`, `"unauthorized"`, `"forbidden"`,
`"not-found"`, `"conflict"`, `"precondition-failed"`, `"internal"`,
`"compile-failed"`, `"publish-gate"`. Absent only on the few
extractor-level rejections that don't flow through `ApiError`.

Optional `compile_errors` carries structured per-edge / per-node failures
from the workflow compiler so the editor can highlight inline.
Optional `failing_tests` carries the publish gate's per-test detail.

failing_tests.description:
Structured per-test failures returned by the publication gate when a
publish is blocked. Absent on non-gate errors. The shape mirrors
`models::template_test::FailingTestInfo`; declared as `Value` here to
avoid pulling the dependency into this module's signature.

EscapeHatch.description:
Flavor-specific raw passthrough. Slurm fills `sbatch_directives`; Nomad fills
`hcl_stanza`. Serialized into `job_template_versions.escape_hatch`.

oneOf.description: Present only for `TokenBridgedOut`.

effect_result.description:
Raw JSON returned by the effect handler (EffectCompleted) or a
failure envelope (EffectFailed). Null for other event types.

oneOf.description:
Present when this event is a signal-injected `TokenCreated` whose
signal_key matches a row in `causality_signal_dispatches` â€” i.e.
we know which effect originally dispatched this signal.

ExecutionBackendType.description:
Discriminator selecting which executor backend handles an automated step.

Snake-case wire values: `"python"`, `"process"`, `"docker"`, `"http"`,
`"llm"`, `"file_ops"`, `"kreuzberg"`, `"surya"`, `"smtp"`,
`"catalogue_query"`.

This is the canonical OpenAPI discriminator, the Y.Doc-stored string in
production templates, and the executor's `ExecutionSpec.backend` value.
Both the mekhan compiler and the executor registry key off it.

entrypoint.description:
Filename of the entrypoint script within the node's staged files.
Backends that don't run a user script (e.g. `http`) ignore this; the
editor still surfaces it for python/process/docker.

FieldKind.description:
Type kind for a typed port field. Superset of `TaskFieldKind`: adds `Bool`
(currently piggybacks on `Checkbox` in human-task forms), `Timestamp`
(needed for trigger fire times and audit fields), and `Json` (opaque
escape hatch for legacy / dynamic payloads). Snake-case wire values.

FieldMapping.description:
A single field mapping for `Trigger.payload_mapping`. Each entry projects
an event scope into a typed token field via a Rhai expression. The compiler
validates that `target_field` exists in the resolved target port and that
`expression` parses.

expression.description:
Rhai expression evaluated against the trigger source's event scope
(`payload`, `fire_time`, etc. â€” varies by source kind).

targetField.description: Name of the target port field this mapping fills.

FileOpsConfig.description: Tagged enum of all file operations.

FileUploadResponse.description:
Response shape for `POST /api/v1/files/upload/{id}/{node_id}`.

The handler returns S3 metadata after a successful upload.

oneOf.description: Spawn: an instance was created. `instance_id` is the new instance.
Signal: at least one running instance received the signal.

Signal had no running instances to send to. Not an error â€” just
reported so the caller knows.
Concurrency policy dropped the fire (`Skip`/`DedupKey`).

`SingleActiveCoalesce`: a sibling instance is still running, so this
fire was *coalesced* â€” recorded as missed, and the dispatcher will
dispatch exactly one follow-up fire after the active instance
terminates. Distinct from `Dropped` because the workflow does see
the missed observation (eventually).

FireOutcome.description:
Result of a single fire attempt. Kept structurally similar across sources
so the history endpoint can render a uniform table.

FireResult.description: Wrap an outcome with metadata the history endpoint records on every fire.

net_parameters.description:
Submitter-supplied net-level parameter bag for the spawned instance.
Threaded into `LoadScenarioRequest.net_parameters` and stored on the
engine's `PetriNetService`, where the firing path consults it for
`$params.` resolution and pre-dispatch metadata (e.g. `tenant_id`).
Opaque, generic infra â€” no domain semantics ascribed here.

payload.description:
JSON object whose top-level keys are bound as the trigger's scope
identifiers for `payload_mapping`. For `Manual` triggers supply the
form values keyed by field name (matching `source_scope`); for other
sources the dispatcher synthesizes this scope from the event itself.

skip_mask.description:
Per-run ablation: transition IDs to skip at evaluate-time.
Threaded through the dispatcher into the engine's
`LoadScenarioRequest.skip_mask` (Î³.mekhan wire). Empty by default
â€” research-harness ablation flows surface this; ordinary fires omit.
#126.2: extends the previously-always-empty trigger-boundary stub.

stage_overrides.description:
Per-run ablation: per-transition JSON merge-patch keyed by
transition_id. Threaded into `LoadScenarioRequest.stage_overrides`.
Same surface + provenance as `skip_mask`. #126.2.

oneOf.description:
Present only on a successful `WaitForResult` fire â€” the instance's
terminal disposition. Absent (not `null`) for FireAndForget, keeping
the default response byte-identical to pre-feature behavior.

FleetMetrics.description:
`GET /api/v1/clusters/metrics` response â€” one [`ClusterMetrics`] per cluster
the caller's workspace touched in-window, plus a `fleet_total` rollup over
the same windowed, workspace-scoped row set.

GuardDiagnosticDto.description: Flattened guard diagnostic (`node_id` is highlighted in the editor).

instance_id.description:
Workflow instance that produced this process (NULL for petri-lab
scenarios created outside a mekhan instance, or unlinked legacy rows).

net_id.description: Engine net id ("mekhan-{instance_id}") for the producing instance.

HttpConfig.description: Configuration for the HTTP request backend.

oneOf.description: Authentication configuration.

auth_resource.description:
Workspace resource alias supplying the auth secret. When set, the
HTTP backend reads `<auth_resource>.json` (ConfigOverlay channel) and
fills the secret of whichever `auth` scheme is selected:
`http_bearer.token` â†’ `Bearer{token}`, `http_basic.{username,password}`
â†’ `Basic{..}`, `http_api_key.{header_name,value}` â†’ `Header{name,value}`.
Per-step inline values and env-var fallbacks still win over the
resource when both are present.

body.description: Inline request body. Mutually exclusive with `body_from_input`.

body_from_input.description: Name of a staged input file whose contents become the request body.

danger_accept_invalid_certs.description: Accept invalid TLS certificates (for dev/self-signed servers).

expected_status_codes.description: Expected success status codes. Empty means 2xx = success.

follow_redirects.description: Whether to follow HTTP redirects (default: true).

headers.description: Request headers. Template substitution supported in values.

max_response_bytes.description: Maximum response body size in bytes (default: 1 MB).

output_mapping.description: Maps declared output names to response field selectors.

query.description: URL query parameters. Template substitution supported in values.

response_mode.description: How to interpret the response body.

ImageDisplay.description:
Layout mode for image blocks. Snake-case wire values: `"single"`,
`"grid"`, `"gallery"`.

ImageInput.description: An image to include with the user prompt.

media_type.description: MIME type. If absent, guessed from file extension.

path.description: Path to the image file (resolved from `{{input:NAME}}`).

InstanceChild.description:
One spawned sub-workflow child run of a parent instance, returned by
`GET /api/v1/instances/{id}/children`. A SubWorkflow node runs its child as
a separate engine net; the causality ingest registers each spawn as a
first-class child `workflow_instances` row (see migration
`20240130000000`). A SubWorkflow inside a Loop/Map spawns one child per
iteration, so multiple rows can share `parent_node_id` â€” ordered by
`spawn_seq` (the spawn order, i.e. iteration order).

id.description: The child instance id (open `/instances/{id}` to drill in).

parent_node_id.description:
The SubWorkflow `WorkflowNode.id` in the parent graph that spawned this
child. Group children by this to attach them to a node on the canvas.

spawn_seq.description:
Parent net's spawn-event sequence; orders sibling children of the same
node (one per Loop/Map iteration).

template_id.description: The child's resolved template id + version (its own graph to render).

InstanceConcurrencyPolicy.description:
Template-level instance concurrency policy. Read by the trigger
dispatcher on fire and the lifecycle listener on instance terminal.

Tagged on the wire as `{"mode": "unlimited"}` / `{"mode":
"single_active_coalesce"}` so future variants (e.g. queue, locked)
can land without breaking the existing wire shape.

InstanceEventsResponse.description:
Response shape for `GET /api/v1/instances/{id}/events`.

Mirrors the literal `json!({ "net_id": ..., "events": [...], "event_count": ... })`
envelope the handler previously emitted. `events` stays `Vec<serde_json::Value>`
because the petri-lab event shape is heterogeneous (one of many event types).

InstanceListItem.description: Instance with template name, returned by list queries (JOIN with workflow_templates).

JobTemplateDetail.description:
Detail view returned by `GET /api/v1/job-templates/{id}`: the template plus
its full version history and current stagings.

JobTemplateSummary.description:
Compact list-row shape. Returned by `GET /api/v1/job-templates` â€” never
carries per-version payload so the list endpoint stays cheap.

JobTemplateVersion.description:
One version, materialized with its decoded JSONB payload. Member of
[`JobTemplateDetail::versions`].

JoinMode.description:
Firing rule for a `Join` node. `All` (the default) is the AND-join â€”
waits for every incoming branch. `Any` fires per arriving token â€” the
canonical petri-net XOR-join, dual of `Decision`'s XOR-split.

KreuzbergConfig.description: Configuration for the Kreuzberg document extraction backend.

file.description:
For single mode: the staged input name containing the file to extract.
Defaults to `"file"` or the sole staged input if there is exactly one.

files.description:
For batch mode: list of input names to extract.
If empty, all staged inputs are used.

force_ocr.description: Force OCR even on text-based PDFs.

mime_type.description: Optional MIME type override. When absent, kreuzberg auto-detects.

oneOf.description: OCR configuration.
PDF-specific options.

LeaseBinding.description:
A binding to a `datacenter` resource for a loop-scoped lease (L3). Lives
under [`WorkflowNodeData::Loop`]'s `lease`; its presence makes `lower_loop`
hoist the claim/grant/register/release handshake to loop scope â€” ONE
allocation held across all iterations, released exactly once on exit.

Mirrors [`DeploymentModel::Scheduled`]'s `scheduler: Option<String>` +
`request: Option<Value>` and [`ResourcePoolBinding`] so the existing
`resolve_binding(..., "datacenter", ...)` + lease-definition machinery
applies unchanged. The field is named `scheduler` (not `alias`) for symmetry
with the `Scheduled` lease path the loop body would otherwise inherit
per-step.

request.description:
Claim-schema-shaped request params (`gpu_count`/`gpu_type`/
`max_duration_secs`); validated against the datacenter kind's
`claim_schema`. `None` â‡’ the allocator's default placement.

scheduler.description:
`datacenter` resource alias (workspace alias) the loop holds a lease
against. Resolved at publish to `pool-<resource_id>` + the
`Lease__datacenter` schema, the same path as `Scheduled.scheduler`
(`resolve_binding("datacenter")`).

LineageResponse.description: Lineage response: artifacts grouped by iteration/step.

LiveArtifactEvent.description:
Emitted when a catalogue entry INSERT succeeds for a process-tagged
artifact. The SSE handler filters by `process_id` and optionally by
`category` / `user_metadata.render_hint` before forwarding to clients.

process_step.description:
HPI tag/annotation propagated from the engine's `EffectCompleted`
event (`process_step_completed` âˆ˜ `process_step_started`). A label
for human-process correlation â€” NOT a pointer to the
`step_execution` table. See module-level docs.

api_key.description: API key. Falls back to provider-specific env var if absent.

images.description:
Images to include with the user prompt.
Each entry references a staged input file path (after `{{input:NAME}}` resolution).

pending.description:
Turns produced since `history` was last persisted â€” for the agent
loop, the tool result (or synthetic feedback) the engine accumulated
on the token between LLM calls. Appended after `history` when
assembling the request; the off-token base (`history`) plus this
delta is what the worker persists as the next turn's cumulative
transcript blob. Empty for single-shot LLM steps.

prompt.description: The user prompt to send to the LLM.

reasoning.description:
Whether the model uses reasoning / chain-of-thought ("thinking").
Maps to Ollama's `think` request parameter. `None` = leave the model's
provider default; `Some(false)` disables reasoning; `Some(true)` forces
it on. Disabling matters for reasoning-capable models (e.g. qwen3.6)
doing structured-output extraction: with reasoning on under a
`format`/json-schema constraint they can loop and generate to the token
cap without emitting valid JSON. Adapters for non-reasoning models
ignore this field.

resource_alias.description:
Optional workspace resource name (e.g. `openai_prod`) the LLM step is
bound to. When set, the compiler emits a ResourceEnvelope borrow that
stages `<resource_alias>.json` into the run dir; the backend overlays
the resource's `api_key` / `base_url` / `organization` on top of any
per-step values, so the step's authoring surface stays clean.

oneOf.description:
Response format constraint. When set to `json_schema`, the provider
will use constrained decoding to guarantee valid JSON output.

tools.description:
Tools the LLM may call. Populated by the agent compiler from
child node `tool_meta` + input ports; empty for single-shot LLM
`AutomatedStep`s. Adapters that don't support tool calls (Ollama
today) ignore this field gracefully.

LlmToolCall.description:
A single tool invocation requested by an LLM in one turn.

Normalized across Anthropic / OpenAI / Ollama adapter shapes. The
`id` is provider-assigned and opaque to the platform; downstream
transitions match on `name` only.

LogsTailResponse.description:
Response shape for `GET /api/v1/processes/{process_id}/logs/tail`.

Frontend reads `body.logs[]` directly â€” keep the single-field envelope.

end.description:
End of the time window (RFC3339 timestamp or unix nanoseconds).

May carry `{{slug.field}}` references. When absent the backend defaults
to "now" (range queries only).

limit.description: Maximum number of entries returned. Defaults to 1000.

operation.description:
Range query vs instant query. Defaults to `QueryRange`. The source of
truth for which Loki HTTP API path the backend targets.

query.description:
The LogQL query.

May carry `{{slug.field}}` references resolved at runtime against the
staged producer envelopes. Interpolated values are escaped for a LogQL
double-quoted string literal so an upstream value cannot break out of a
matcher.

resource_alias.description:
Which workspace `loki` resource binds the connection. Required â€”
this is the connection binding; the compiler errors if absent. The
resolved resource (base_url/token/org_id) is overlaid into the config
before execution.

since.description:
Relative look-back duration used when `start`/`end` are absent, e.g.
`"5m"`, `"1h"` (range queries only).

start.description:
Start of the time window (RFC3339 timestamp or unix nanoseconds).

May carry `{{slug.field}}` references. When absent the backend derives
the start from `since` (range queries only).

step.description: Query resolution step for metric range queries, e.g. `"30s"`.

timeout_ms.description:
Per-request timeout in milliseconds. Defaults to 30000.

Capped at the job-level `RunContext.timeout`.

LokiDirection.description:
Search direction for log queries.

`Backward` (the default) returns the newest entries first â€” the usual
"tail" view. `Forward` returns oldest-first. Passed through to Loki as the
`direction` query parameter.

LokiOperation.description:
Whether the step runs a range query or an instant query.

`QueryRange` (the default) hits `/loki/api/v1/query_range` over a time
window â€” the usual mode for log streams. `Query` hits
`/loki/api/v1/query` for an instant query at a single point in time
(typically a metric query). This is the source of truth for which Loki
HTTP API path the backend targets.

LoopAccumulator.description:
One fold/scan slot on a [`WorkflowNodeData::Loop`]. Lives as an additional
field in the loop's parked `p_<id>_data` envelope (the iteration counter
generalized): `init` is evaluated once in the enter transition, `merge_expr`
is re-evaluated write-once-per-iteration in the continue transition. Both
are Rhai expressions. `merge_expr` may reference the prior accumulator value
as `<loop_slug>.<var>` and the body's output as `<body_slug>.<field>` â€” the
standard read-arc synthesis resolves those borrows automatically.

init.description:
Rhai expression evaluated in the enter transition scope (the entering
workflow token is bound as `input`). Keep simple â€” e.g. `"0"`, `"[]"`,
`"#{}"`.

mergeExpr.description:
Rhai expression evaluated in the continue transition scope, producing the
next accumulator value. References the prior value as `<loop_slug>.<var>`
and body output as `<body_slug>.<field>`.

var.description:
Rhai identifier the accumulator is addressed by, both inside the loop's
own continue transition (`<loop_slug>.<var>`) and downstream. Must not
be `iteration` (reserved) and must be unique within the loop.

form.description:
Form schema for the "Run with parameters" dialog. Reuses the same
`TaskFieldConfig` shape as human-task forms.

MergeStrategy.description:
How a `Join { mode: All }` merges the tokens arriving on its joined
branches.

`ShallowLastWins` is the historical behaviour (top-level keys overwrite,
last branch to arrive wins on a key collision). `DeepMerge` recursively
merges nested object values instead of overwriting them.

ModelRef.description:
LLM model + provider selection for an [`WorkflowNodeData::Agent`]. Mirrors
the subset of `aithericon_executor_backend_configs::llm::LlmConfig` the
editor authors directly (provider, model, optional creds / sampling
knobs); the degenerate single-turn lowering reconstructs the full
`LlmConfig` from these fields plus the Agent's prompts. Wire shape
matches the existing `LlmConfig` JSON one-for-one so the equivalence
test (PR 1) produces byte-identical `config_ref` blobs.

provider.description:
`"openai"` | `"anthropic"` | `"ollama"`. Wire format is lowercase to
line up with `LlmConfig::Provider`'s `rename_all = "lowercase"`.

resourceAlias.description:
Workspace resource alias the LLM call binds to (e.g. `"openai_prod"`).
Same channel as `LlmConfig::resource_alias` â€” the compiler emits a
`ResourceEnvelope` borrow when present.

MultipartFileUpload.description:
Multipart body wrapper for spec documentation. The runtime extractor is
`axum::extract::Multipart`; this struct only exists so the spec shows the
expected field name and type.

file.description: Binary file contents (one of the allowed mime types).

on.description: Which terminal status counts as a fire.

sourceTemplateId.description: Source template whose instance completion fires this trigger.

sourceVersion.description: Specific version, or `None` for any published version.

NodeDescriptor.description:
Frontend-visible metadata for one node type. Returned by
`GET /api/v1/node-types`.

Mirrors [`crate::backends::BackendDescriptor`]'s role for backends. The
Svelte component map, Lucide icon imports, and per-section property
panels stay frontend-only (they reference Svelte components that can't
be serialized through JSON); everything else flows from here.

kind.description:
Runtime kind as snake_case string. Differs from `wire_name` only for
`agent` (kind = `automated_step`).

wireName.description: Snake-case wire tag â€” matches the variant's serde rename.

OutputAuthoring.description:
Who owns the AutomatedStep's output port shape â€” the user (free
authoring), the backend (fixed canonical shape), or the backend's
